tasks.title: WCAG AA color contrast audit and fix across all components
tasks.description:
Audit all Tailwind color pairings for WCAG AA compliance (4.5:1 ratio for normal text, 3:1 for large text) and fix violations. Focus on light mode first (dark mode is generally good).

**Known violations to fix:**
1. `text-slate-400` (#94a3b8) on white/light backgrounds — ratio ~3.5:1, fails AA. Found in:
   - `apps/web/src/components/shared/FilterBar.tsx` — inactive pill text
   - `apps/web/src/components/ActivityFeed.tsx` — timestamp text
   - `apps/web/src/components/CommentSection.tsx` — timestamp text
   - `apps/web/src/components/shared/SearchInput.tsx` — search icon
   Fix: Replace `text-slate-400` with `text-slate-500` (ratio ~4.6:1) for text content, keeping `text-slate-400` only for decorative borders/dividers where contrast isn't required.

2. `text-slate-500` (#64748b) on `bg-slate-100` (#f1f5f9) — ratio ~4.8:1, borderline. Found in:
   - `apps/web/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx` line 8 — slate column pill
   - `apps/web/src/components/SprintSection.tsx` line 15 — low priority badge
   - `apps/web/src/components/SprintSection.tsx` line 30 — normal due date
   Fix: Replace with `text-slate-700` (#334155) on `bg-slate-100` for solid 7:1+ ratio.

3. Audit all status/priority color badges — `bg-*-100 text-*-700` pairings in KanbanBoard.tsx (column accents), TaskDetailPanel.tsx (tool categories), SprintSection.tsx (priority + due date badges). These are likely fine but verify.

4. Check `tailwind.config.js` status/priority colors: `status.todo` (slate-400), `priority.low` (slate-400) — if these are used as text colors on white, they fail. Search for where these semantic colors are applied.

5. Dark mode: Verify `text-slate-400` on `dark:bg-slate-800/900` — should be ~6:1 (passes). Spot-check 3-4 components.

Acceptance criteria: No `text-slate-400` used for readable text content (timestamps, labels, descriptions) — only for decorative elements. All text-on-background pairings meet 4.5:1. `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` pass.

tasks.title: Spacing/typography normalization across key pages and components
tasks.description:
Audit and normalize spacing and typography scale across the app's key pages and components. The goal is consistent use of Tailwind spacing utilities — not a redesign, just fixing obvious inconsistencies.

**Established patterns (from Modal.tsx, shared components):**
- Section padding: `px-4 py-3` or `px-5 py-4` for containers
- Item padding: `px-2 py-1.5` or `px-3 py-2` for list items/rows
- Gaps: `gap-1` tight, `gap-2` standard, `gap-4` section-level
- Font sizes: `text-xs` labels, `text-sm` body, `text-base` or `text-lg` headings

**Pages to audit:**
1. `apps/web/src/pages/Home.tsx` — landing page. Check heading hierarchy (h1/h2/h3 sizes), spacing between sections, CTA button sizing.
2. `apps/web/src/pages/Projects.tsx` — project list cards. Normalize card padding, gap between cards, text sizes for project name vs description.
3. `apps/web/src/components/TaskDetailPanel.tsx` — task detail sidebar. This is a dense panel — check that section headers use consistent size, field labels use consistent style, spacing between sections is uniform.
4. `apps/web/src/components/BacklogView.tsx` — backlog task rows. Check row padding consistency, sprint section headers vs task rows.

**What to fix:**
- Mixed padding scales at the same semantic level (e.g., some cards use `p-3`, siblings use `p-4`)
- Inconsistent heading sizes (e.g., section headers alternating between `text-sm font-semibold` and `text-base font-medium`)
- Irregular gaps in flex/grid containers
- Don't change functional layout — just normalize the scale

Acceptance criteria: Consistent spacing scale across audited pages. No visual regressions (layout looks the same, just more uniform). `pnpm build` passes.

tasks.title: Migrate file attachments from local disk to S3-compatible object storage
tasks.description:
Replace the current multer local disk storage with S3-compatible object storage (works with AWS S3, Cloudflare R2, or MinIO). This is critical because Railway has ephemeral filesystem — uploads are lost on deploy/restart.

**1. Install S3 SDK:**
`cd apps/api && pnpm add @aws-sdk/client-s3 @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`

**2. Create S3 utility** `apps/api/src/utils/s3.ts`:
- Initialize `S3Client` from env vars: `S3_ENDPOINT`, `S3_BUCKET`, `S3_REGION`, `S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`
- Export helpers: `uploadToS3(key, body, contentType)`, `getSignedDownloadUrl(key, expiresIn)`, `deleteFromS3(key)`
- Use `PutObjectCommand`, `GetObjectCommand` (for presigned URLs via `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`), `DeleteObjectCommand`
- Key format: `attachments/${orgId}/${taskId}/${timestamp}-${sanitizedFilename}`

**3. Modify upload route** `apps/api/src/routes/upload.ts`:
- Change multer to use `memoryStorage()` instead of `diskStorage()` — file available as `req.file.buffer`
- On POST: upload buffer to S3 via `uploadToS3()`, store S3 key in `fileKey` DB column
- On GET: generate presigned URL via `getSignedDownloadUrl()` and redirect (302) — no file streaming from disk
- On DELETE: call `deleteFromS3()` alongside DB record deletion
- Remove local `uploads/` directory creation logic
- Keep all existing security (MIME whitelist, file size limit, rate limiting, auth, sanitization)

**4. Update env config:**
- Add S3 vars to `apps/api/.env.example`: `S3_ENDPOINT`, `S3_BUCKET`, `S3_REGION`, `S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`
- Add fallback: if `S3_BUCKET` is not set, log a warning and fall back to local disk storage (for local dev without S3)

**5. No Prisma schema changes needed** — `fileKey` column already stores the key, just changes from local filename to S3 path.

**6. No frontend changes needed** — web UI uses REST endpoints which stay the same.

Acceptance criteria: Upload/download/delete work with S3 env vars set. Local disk fallback works when S3 vars are missing. `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm build` pass. Verify `pnpm typecheck` passes — add `@types/*` or declare module in a `.d.ts` if needed for the AWS SDK.
tasks.reviewNotes: CLAUDE.md includes the full swarm worker role instructions (everything from the swarm-role HTML comment to the end). Only the S3 env var update line is legitimate. Remove the worker role section from CLAUDE.md, keep only the env var change. Re-commit and mark completed. All other code looks great.

tasks.title: Health check S3 connectivity and document backup/monitoring strategy
tasks.description:
Wire S3 connectivity into the existing health check and document the deployment monitoring strategy.

**1. Health check update** `apps/api/src/routes/health.ts` (or wherever GET /api/health is defined — check `apps/api/src/app.ts`):
- Add S3 connectivity check: attempt `HeadBucketCommand` on the configured bucket
- Report in health response: `{ db: 'ok', s3: 'ok'|'not configured'|'error: ...' }`
- Don't fail the entire health check if S3 is not configured (local dev)

**2. Add S3 env vars to deployment docs:**
- Update `CLAUDE.md` env files section to mention S3 vars
- Update `apps/api/.env.example` if not already done by task-003

**3. Document deployment strategy** in `.claude-knowledge/decisions.md`:
- Why S3/R2 over local disk (Railway ephemeral FS)
- Recommended providers: Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible, free egress), AWS S3 (standard), MinIO (self-hosted)
- DB backup: Railway provides automated daily backups with point-in-time recovery — document how to access/restore
- Monitoring: recommend Uptime Robot free tier for `/api/health` endpoint polling
- Railway alerting: document available built-in alerting for restart loops, memory, CPU

Acceptance criteria: Health endpoint reports S3 status. Decisions doc updated with deployment strategy. `pnpm typecheck && pnpm build` pass.
tasks.reviewNotes: Blocked on task-003 CLAUDE.md fix. Once task-003 is re-submitted cleanly, task-004 changes (health check + decisions.md) look good and can be merged together.

tasks.title: PWA service worker with vite-plugin-pwa for offline caching
tasks.description:
Add a production-ready PWA service worker using vite-plugin-pwa for offline caching and install prompts.

**1. Install plugin:**
`cd apps/web && pnpm add -D vite-plugin-pwa`

**2. Configure in Vite** `apps/web/vite.config.ts`:
```typescript
import { VitePWA } from 'vite-plugin-pwa'

plugins: [
  react(),
  VitePWA({
    registerType: 'autoUpdate',
    workbox: {
      globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,svg,png,ico,woff2}'],
      runtimeCaching: [
        {
          urlPattern: /^\/api\//,
          handler: 'NetworkFirst',
          options: {
            cacheName: 'api-cache',
            expiration: { maxEntries: 50, maxAgeSeconds: 300 },
          },
        },
      ],
    },
    manifest: false, // Use existing manifest.json
  }),
]
```
- Use `NetworkFirst` for API calls (fresh data preferred, cached fallback)
- Cache static assets with `CacheFirst` (default workbox behavior for precache)
- Set `manifest: false` since `public/manifest.json` already exists

**3. Update manifest.json** `apps/web/public/manifest.json`:
- Add `start_url: "/"`, `scope: "/"`
- Verify icon sizes (192x192 and 512x512 already present)
- Add `categories: ["productivity", "project-management"]`

**4. Add offline fallback page** (optional but nice):
- vite-plugin-pwa can generate an offline fallback automatically
- Or create a minimal `apps/web/public/offline.html` with "You are offline" message

Acceptance criteria: `pnpm build` generates a service worker in dist/. Manifest is valid. `pnpm typecheck` passes. Verify `pnpm typecheck` passes — add `@types/*` or declare module in a `.d.ts` if needed for vite-plugin-pwa.

tasks.title: E2E supertest export tests + API shared-types import + composite og:image
tasks.description:
Three Wave 19 follow-up items bundled together:

**1. E2E supertest export tests** — Add HTTP-level tests for the export REST endpoints that are currently only verified via DB queries.

Install supertest: `cd apps/api && pnpm add -D supertest @types/supertest`

Create `apps/api/src/__tests__/export.integration.test.ts`:
- Import `app` from `../app.ts` (the Express app, not the server listener)
- Use supertest to make HTTP requests: `request(app).get('/api/export/project/:id/json').set('Authorization', 'Bearer <token>')`
- Test cases:
  1. GET `/api/export/project/:projectId/json` — verify 200, JSON body contains project name + tasks array
  2. GET `/api/export/project/:projectId/csv` — verify 200, Content-Type is text/csv, body contains CSV headers
  3. GET `/api/export/project/:projectId/activity/json` — verify 200, JSON array of activities
  4. GET without auth — verify 401
  5. GET with wrong org's project — verify 404 or 403 (tenant isolation)
- Setup: create user + org + project + task in beforeEach (same pattern as other integration tests)
- Generate a valid JWT for the test user using `jose` (import `SignJWT` from 'jose', sign with `JWT_SECRET`)

**2. API shared-types import** — Add `@tasktoad/shared-types` as a dependency to `apps/api/package.json`:
- `pnpm --filter api add @tasktoad/shared-types@workspace:*`
- In a few key resolver files (e.g., `resolvers/task.ts`, `resolvers/project.ts`), add type imports from shared-types where they improve clarity. Don't refactor all resolvers — just demonstrate the pattern in 2-3 places with a comment noting the shared type.

**3. Composite og:image** — Create a proper 1200x630 social preview image:
- Create `apps/web/public/og-image.svg` at 1200x630 with brand dark background (#1B2631), a centered frog/toad icon in brand green (#4CAF50), and "TaskToad" text + tagline below. Use SVG text elements for the text.
- Update `apps/web/index.html` og:image meta tag to point to `/og-image.svg`

Acceptance criteria: Supertest export tests pass (`pnpm --filter api test`). API imports shared-types. og:image meta tag points to new 1200x630 image. `pnpm typecheck && pnpm build` pass. Verify `pnpm typecheck` passes — add `@types/*` or declare module in a `.d.ts` if needed for supertest.
